descriptions: Added a brand new Emote Editor (/emoteeditor) — a full in-game editor for managing and creating emotes, with live position/offset editing that applies instantly without restarts.
The Emote Editor includes a built-in Emote Creator for Solo and Shared (2-ped) emotes, replacing the removed /emotecreator command.
Added Import/Export — copy any emote's config as ready-to-paste code, or paste code in bulk to add/update emotes.
Added custom categories — create or delete your own (deleted categories move their emotes back to Emotes).
Added search and select-all to find emotes fast and select every match in a category at once.
Added the ability to rename /e commands; the old command name keeps working too.
The editor is format-aware: edits regular emotes, walks, expressions, and weapon styles.
Added shared emote support — adjust partner offsets and jump straight to your partner from the editor.
Added a Quick Start guide that auto-shows the first time, with examples and a docs link.
Added a 3D gizmo for prop placement in the Emote Creator — drag handles to place/rotate props instead of typing slider numbers. Works on every prop slot in both Solo and Shared modes.
Added a 3D gizmo for /animpos — press G to position your ped's animation with the gizmo. Right-drag to orbit, scroll to zoom, E confirm, Esc cancel. Default mode is per-player and saved.
Added Config.AnimAdjustGizmo to disable the /animpos gizmo server-wide.
Hold Shift while dragging any gizmo to snap to clean steps (5 cm / 15°).
Removed the /emotecreator command — Emote Creator now lives inside /emoteeditor → Create.
Fixed the Emote Creator sometimes not deleting the old prop when changing props (props could pile up) — props now spawn cleanly via an Apply button.
